title: Remake Sequel Discussion
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Remake / Sequel Discussion             Quote Post by michaelovan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:26 pm I just got back from EVA 3.0 and, while my Japanese is by no means fluent, SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:30 pm Thanks for that. Now I'm waiting for LiLi's two cents

[]: Re: Remake / Sequel Discussion (SPOILERS)             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:31 pm michaelovan wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:33 pm Thanks a lot. I'm waiting for some other comments from people who saw it that could clarify this thing. It looks interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by michaelovan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:38 pm Yeah, I would love for another set of eyes and ears!

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:43 pm He says something to the effect of this (third impact) having happened before, alluding to millions upon millions of skulls resting on the earth, far below the Geofront.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:53 pm Jayfive wrote: Based on what has been said everywhere, those are Eva skulls. Too big to be human.

[]:             Quote Post by michaelovan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:55 pm Jayfive wrote: Based on what has been said everywhere, those are Eva skulls. Too big to be human.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:01 pm SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:05 pm Or they could have fucked up proportions even though they're working with digital animation, who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Choo1701 » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:06 pm Reichu wrote: Don't look a whole lot like Eva heads. Evas have really narrow heads with eyes spaced further to the sides of the head and proportionately bigger teeth. Then, they did make the mistake of giving a prototype Eva an effectively homonoid skull in EoE, so maybe I'm giving khara too much credit here.

[]:             Quote Post by Grub » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:07 pm Maybe they're all skulls from reincarnations of the same eva-sized entity?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:07 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Or they could have fucked up proportions even though they're working with digital animation, who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:18 pm Choo1701 wrote: Well using human skulls (lots of skulls by the looks of it) is a lot more symbolic, and easier for the audience to connect too than whatever an Evangelion skull looks like.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:22 pm But isnt that only based on data from NGE? We've not seen anything in rebuild to confirm this AFAICR. I'll happily plump for 'eva-sized beings' for now :)

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Nov 17, 2012 1:49 pm From what I've seen so far, it has occurred to me that it's possible that the series is neither a perfect loop (a "groundhog day") nor a direct sequel (because that's stupid) but an imperfect time loop where certain events resonate with each other (Bardiel still happens, but the circumstances are different) and junk from the last iteration survives or manifests somehow (all of the clues/references) or is drawn into being by a sort of existential inertia. If this is the case, I don't expect a direct, explicit confirmation.

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Sat Nov 17, 2012 2:01 pm This has been my interpretation pretty much from the beginning - NGE happened, in another timeline/dimension/whatever, and "echoes" of those events influence the current Rebuild reality. I get lumped with the silly literalist sequel theorists anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Nov 17, 2012 2:01 pm Something that's been vaguely acknowledged here and there but never really discussed outright is the possibility that the NTE has nothing to do with NGE/EoE but is still a loop within its own right. I mean, I don't really see evidence for that, either (apart from some of Kaworu's lines), but it's nevertheless possible to have repeating events without need for a sequel proper.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Nov 17, 2012 2:09 pm Kodaemon wrote: This has been my interpretation pretty much from the beginning - NGE happened, in another timeline/dimension/whatever, and "echoes" of those events influence the current Rebuild reality. I get lumped with the silly literalist sequel theorists anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Gekusu » Sat Nov 17, 2012 2:39 pm Regarding the skulls: SPOILER: Show
